message:
feat(policy): Cx-first send_text + dispatch helpers (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Begin Cx-first migration of policy.rs's PolicyGatedInjector.
This subsystem had 0 of 5 public async send methods with cx
siblings despite being the primary surface between policy
evaluation and actual wezterm subprocess dispatch — the one
place where caller cancellation should reach the terminal.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated where
noted):

  * `PolicyGatedInjector::dispatch_wezterm_send(action, pane_id,
    text)` — shared helper extracted from the legacy `inject`'s
    ActionKind → WeztermInterface match. Returns `Result<(),
    crate::Error>`. UNCONDITIONAL (no cx feature gate) so the
    legacy inject can use it too. Refactors inject to call this
    helper instead of inlining the match — strictly equivalent
    output for every branch (verified by all existing policy
    tests still passing).

  * `PolicyGatedInjector::dispatch_wezterm_send_with_cx(cx,
    action, pane_id, text)` (asupersync-runtime-gated) —
    Cx-first parallel. Routes each ActionKind to its `_with_cx`
    WeztermInterface method (tick 44/45 trait extensions), so
    caller cancellation propagates into the underlying
    `wezterm cli` subprocess.

  * `PolicyGatedInjector::inject_with_cx(cx, pane_id, text,
    action, actor, capabilities, workflow_id)`
    (asupersync-runtime-gated) — Cx-first parallel of `inject`.
    Mirrors every stage of the legacy inject (policy eval,
    decision capture, trauma feedback, ingress tap, audit
    emission) but dispatches the wezterm send via
    `dispatch_wezterm_send_with_cx`. All other stages remain on
    the ambient path — they're either synchronous (policy eval,
    decision capture) or short storage writes (audit). The
    single cx-aware seam is the wezterm send, which is where
    long-running subprocess latency actually lives.

  * `PolicyGatedInjector::send_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, text,
    actor, capabilities, workflow_id)` — public Cx-first entry
    point. Delegates to `inject_with_cx` with
    `ActionKind::SendText`, mirroring the legacy `send_text` →
    `inject` delegation.

Regression test (new) `send_text_with_cx_matches_legacy_deny_path`:

  * Uses PolicyEngine::strict() and PaneCapabilities with
    alt_screen=Some(true) — exactly the topology from the
    existing `injector_policy_context_marks_mux_surface` test.
    That capability triggers the strict engine's
    `policy.alt_screen` rule.
  * Drives `send_text_with_cx(&cx, 1, "echo hi", ActorKind::Robot,
    &caps, None)`.
  * Asserts:
    - result is Denied with action == SendText.
    - decision.rule_id() == Some("policy.alt_screen") (same
      rule as legacy).
    - decision.context().surface == PolicySurface::Mux (same
      surface marker as legacy).

This test exercises the full `send_text_with_cx` →
`inject_with_cx` flow through the Deny branch, proving the
policy evaluation + ingress tap + audit emission paths all
work the same on the Cx-first path as the legacy. The Allow
branch (where dispatch_wezterm_send_with_cx actually fires)
isn't exercised here because PolicyEngine::strict with
alt_screen=true guarantees Deny; testing the Allow branch
requires a real WeztermClient + mock, which is a larger
follow-on.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * policy:: — 455 passed / 0 failed (tick 71: 454 -> tick 72:
    455, +1 new parity test; all 108 legacy policy tests in this
    module still green — proves the dispatch_wezterm_send
    extraction preserved semantics bit-for-bit)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (35.21s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first policy.rs progress:
  * `send_text`     ✓ (tick 72, this commit)
  * `send_ctrl_c`   — pending
  * `send_ctrl_d`   — pending
  * `send_ctrl_z`   — pending
  * `send_control`  — pending

Subsequent ticks are now trivial delegates: each remaining
`send_*_with_cx` just calls `inject_with_cx` with the matching
ActionKind. Follows the tick-41/42/43 pattern where tick-40's
helper landing made the rest mechanical.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
